## Using predefined dtypes to reduce memory usage
#### Calender data has 1969 rows with 14 columns, prices has 68,41,121 rows with 4 columns and sales has 30490 rows with 1919 columns
## Starting the EDA with Sales Data
#### Each entry in sales data corresponds to sales quantity of each item in a store across all days
#### For eg: the first row coreesponds to item 1 of hobbies_1 deparment in state california in store 1

#### We also saw that sales data has 1919 columns, among which 6 columns are different type of ids and other 1913 columns are days from d_1 to d_1913. The sales date starts from 29-Jan-2011 and ends on 24-April-2016
#### We can see that FOODS_3 sell the highest values of sales
#### CA_3 sell the most, WI_2 started off low, but it had a sudden increase in level in ending of 1st quarter in 2012
#### WI_3 went downhill at the begining of 2013, CA_2 had a decreasing trend throughout 2014 but increasing trend in 2015
#### We can see that the sales fall to zero just before january, this is because walmart is closed on christmas
## Ending the EDA of sales, starting the EDA of calender data
#### This data contains the details regarding events on each day and it also shows on which days SNAP purchases are allowed
#### We can see that SNAP follows a certain pattern in 3 respective states, In CA, SNAP is allowed on first ten days, TX follows pattern 101-011, WI follows pattern 011
#### There are 162 rows where event_name_1 is not null and only 5 rows where event_name_2 is not null
#### There are total of 31 unique events which belong to 5 unique types, and as we saw before 162 rows, the data given is of 5 years, so these events occur every year
## Calendar data EDA done, starting EDA on prices data
#### Sell prices lie between 0 and 25, where most of them lies between 0 and 10
#### Here we have viewed the price range of all departments

#### Plotted WI and CA Plot in different period as saw anomaly earlier, we can see that there's a change in level of data, interesting part is that both are around June and in both FOODS_3 and FOOS_2 have increasing sales

### Feature Engineering is a process of extracting useful features from existing raw data using maths,statistics and domain knowledge.

#### Feature Engineering is one of the most important steps to complete before starting a Machine Learning analysis.Most of the basic Feature Engineering techniques consist of finding inconsistencies in the data and of creating new features by combining/diving existing ones. Creating the best possible Machine Learning/Deep Learning model can certainly help to achieve good results, but choosing the right features in the right format to feed in a model can by far boost performances leading to the following benefits:

#### In this kernel notebook I am going to cover an exhaustive Feature Engineering topics. I hope you like this kernel and appreciate to leave your suggestions /comments and please do <font color ='red'>UPVOTE</font> if you like this notebook.
### Data in real world are rarely clean and homogeneous. Typically this is because of the following reasons

#### Why do outliers exist in a dataset?
## <font color="green">Outlier Detection & Removal using IQR(Inter Quartile Range)
#### Now let us visualise median and quantiles for Age feature.Below illustration shows 25%, 50% and 75% are nothing but 25th quantile, median and 75th quantile respectively
#### Let's calculate the boundaries outside which sit the outliers assuming Age follows a Gaussian distribution
#### The upper boundary for Age is 73-74 years. The lower boundary is meaningless as there can't be negative age. This value could be generated due to the lack of normality of the data.
#### Now let's use the IQR(Inter Quantile Range) to calculate the boundaries
#### The boundary using 1.5 times the interquantile range coincides roughly with the boundary determined using the Gaussian distribution (64 vs 71 years).
#### The boundary value using the 3 times the interquantile is a bit high according to normal human life expectancy, particularly in the days of the Titanic.
#### Now let us find out whether there are any outliers according to the above boundaries:
#### Before that let us first remove the passengers with missing data for Age feature.
#### Now finally here are the outliers
#### So clearly it is indicative that the above are the outliers . Based on the above observation it is evident that the majority of the outliers did not survive.
#### Now let us remove the outliers
### So finally after elimination of the outliers we are left with the above set of records in the dataset
#### There we go with remaining dataset after eliminating the outliers
## <font color="green">Outlier Detection & Removal using Z score
### Z score indicates how many standard deviation away a data point is.
#### Get data points that has z score higher than 3 or lower than -3. Another way of saying same thing is get data points that are more than 3 standard deviation away
#### So above are the list of final outliers with data points more than 3 standard deviations away.
#### Now let us remove these outliers
### So finally we were able to remove two outliers using this Z score approach

### 这是M5 forecast比赛,这里学习的是M5 Forecast: Poisson Loss (top 10%)的方案,经过我的改进在排行榜上能达到395名,参赛人数为5582名,即0.0707631673。
### 1.导入必要的python库。固定随机种子,保证模型可以复现。
#### 这里有5个数据集,下面我们一个一个来看看。

### 2.1   calendar是日历文件。date是日期,wm_yr_wk是周的编号,因为每个相同的wm_yr_wk在数据中有7个。weekday和wday都是星期几,month是月,year是年,d是从训练数据开头开始的天数,总共有将近2000天。event_name和event_type可能是节假日,因此数据中大部分都是缺失值,snap_CA、snap_TX,snap_WI是其他的特征。
#### 这里先对日期数据进行处理,由于这里注释已经写的很详细了,这里不多解释。
#### 这两个文件要一起看,validation是day1到day1913预测接下来28天,这也是公榜的成绩;evaluation是day1到day1941预测接下来28天,这也是私榜的成绩。由于evaluation已经给出了公榜的target,所以公榜前排都是0。除了每天的数据以外,剩下都是一些id。
### 2.3 这是价格的表,就是在第wm_yr_wk周在store_id买item_id的价格为sell_price。
#### 特征工程就是下面的这段代码,看起来代码量不多,特征也是熟悉的滞后特征和滑动窗口。不知道为什么效果这么好。
#### 这里是将数据划分为训练集,验证集和测试集,这里的代码和特征工程关系不大,更多的是对数据进行一些处理,例如rename,drop。这里的数据是时序数据,但是他这里随机划分,所以肯定存在着数据泄露,这也是为什么他在验证集上的分数为0.
#### 这里我没有见过的参数就是'objective': 'poisson',这是使用泊松回归,适用于计数数据的回归问题。
### 5.模型的推理和预测结果的保存。

## What is interesting about this approach?

### This is actually the main difference with the regular approach. We are deriving features to explain the characteristics of the entire series up until that point in time instead of deriving features that are describing the characteristics of one single day.
## What is the main advantage of using this approach?

#### It is a day-by-day model -- consists of 28 seperate models-- so we have the flexibility to modify models for different days. At the same time, training is so fast that we can quickly try different ideas and see how the model performance reacts to it. I think this flexibility and pace is a rare and valuable combination by looking at the public kernels.

### *The stores in California seems to have more variance in their sales as compared to stores in Texas and Wisconsin.*
#### *Checking first 15 time-series we observe that the first time-series is NON-STATIONARY while the rest are STATIONARY.*
#### *The first time-series has now become Stationary.*
#### *Observing Partial Auto Correlation and Auto Correlation we choose p=1 and q=1*
#### *p-value for auto regression and moving average coefficient is less than 0.05 which shows that we can go for higher model with higher lag.*

#### *p-value of LLR test is less than 0.05. Also from summary we can observe that log-likelihood(model2)>log-likelihood(model1), AIC(model2)< AIC(model1) and BIC(model2)< BIC(model1). Hence we go for second model.*
